title: Morronea guatemalensis
taxonomy: Morronea guatemalensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Guatemala. The classification of Morronea guatemalensis includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, order as Asterales, and family as Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Morronea. At the level of species, Morronea guatemalensis is the only recognized species within the genus.

intro: Morronea guatemalensis, also known as Guatemalan Morronea, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Morronea guatemalensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Morronea guatemalensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
